title: strychnos curtisii
intro: Strychnos curtisii is a species of flowering plant in the family Loganiaceae, commonly known as a poisonous plant native to Southeast Asia. The plant's medicinal properties are derived from its seeds, which contain a toxic alkaloid called strychnine, although the seeds are also used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. In traditional medicine, Strychnos curtisii is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or powder for medicinal use, although it's essential to exercise extreme caution when handling the plant due to its toxic properties.
